textByHash.c2f600b05105238535983b0c9a2c511bfc286161: Do you know any other tortoise trainers?
textByHash.1c228a6b32029ae7c96c520c25da19dabff678c8: Tortoises are naturally pacifistic animals that eat nothing but cabbages. I am required to beat those peaceful tendencies out of the softies and turn them into hardened battle-trained killers.
textByHash.b261bcc4ac730fe300008118f0b4c9572fcbc1b1: Firstly I acclimatise them to excessive aural stimulation and simple physical confrontation.
textByHash.7b23b3ad5865cc0195103da21504cae81feb7f7a: Of course. Then I re-educate them to become comfortable with riders and military combat situations.
textByHash.1180550685406ed6503622fc95235f42551c04c3: This is War! Fairness is a luxury of the weak.
textByHash.69eb7ce50f7e5f1679cb20e270aab3bbba30c762: Isn't it obvious? Surely even to your untrained eye that giant tortoises are the perfect mobile armoured field unit?
textByHash.c3b51fd1a8536c832b3c83c37f18979acf932584: They are sturdy animals, and well protected by their shell. They can even swim! All we need to do is put a bit of fire in their bellies.
textByHash.82ee2f51bafac103507fa91733f6d07dd6da5723: We breed them. The originals were found by gnome explorers back in King Healthorg the Great's rule. On their discovery King Healthorg authorised a small party to go there and transport a breeding pair back here. He
textByHash.32967c6dfdf3f2643b5cbb7802de77977719328c: kept one for himself to ride into battle.
textByHash.7a9e057408f0e825b51ecd885b9c0464c88cfa74: Trouble is they take a lot of skill and time to breed, so we're only just getting them battle ready.
